item.title: Why Modi Wants Indians To Stop Buying Gold
item.description: India has more than doubled import tariffs on gold just days after Prime Minister Narendra Modi asked citizens to stop buying the metal for at least a year.

item.title: Gilts Become ‘Half-Hour Trade’ on Politics, HSBC’s Kettner Says
item.description: The UK’s fractious politics is making investors wary of long-term wagers in the country’s gilt markets, according to HSBC Holdings Plc’s Max Kettner.

item.title: Undersea Internet Cable Projects Are Getting Tangled in the Iran War
item.description: If construction ever resumes in the Persian Gulf, tech companies will have a new headache to deal with: Unexploded missiles and mines.

item.title: Equinor to Talk With Germany on Buying Oil From Costlier Wells
item.description: Norway’s biggest energy producer is to hold talks with major European consumers on whether they would support projects to recover costlier oil and gas supplies.

item.title: Jack Ma-Backed Ant’s Profit Fell 79% on AI, Health Care Costs
item.description: Ant Group Co.’s quarterly profit continued to decline after the company increased investment in artificial intelligence technology for its health care, large language model and payment services.

item.title: Adani Power Seeks 80 Billion Rupees in Debt to Fund Expansion
item.description: Adani Power Ltd., India’s largest private thermal power producer, plans to raise 80 billion rupees ($836 million) through local debt markets this year to fund expansion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Investors Will Have Plenty on the Line When Trump Meets Xi
item.description: Donald Trump’s summit with Xi Jinping could have lasting ramifications for markets.

item.title: Engineering Firm TSK Shares Gain in Spain After €150 Million IPO
item.description: Spanish engineering firm TSK Electronica y Electricidad SA’s shares opened higher after the firm’s owners raised €150 million ($176 million) in an initial public offering, the first sizable main market listing in Spain this year.

item.title: Even Silicon Valley’s Congressman Wants to Rein in AI
item.description: Ro Khanna spent years cheering on the tech industry and building ties with wealthy donors. Now he’s pitching himself as a presidential contender with a wealth tax and tough talk about the “Epstein class.”

item.title: Carney’s Trade Push Beyond the US Is Just a Gold Rush, For Now
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney set an ambitious target last year for the country to double its non-US exports over the next decade, a goal that built on his election campaign pledge to make Canada’s economy less reliant on its southern neighbor.

item.title: Lake Tahoe Power Crunch Shows AI’s Growing Energy Toll in West
item.description: Lake Tahoe has long been a refuge for Silicon Valley’s tech elite, from Mark Zuckerberg to Larry Ellison. Now the artificial intelligence boom behind much of that wealth is straining the region’s power market, pushing up costs for residents in the lakeside towns below.

item.title: Alibaba Posts First Operating Loss Since 2021 After AI Splurge
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. posted its first operating loss since the depths of the Covid pandemic, underscoring the extent to which China’s e-commerce leader is spending on the AI initiatives it hopes will power its next phase of growth.

item.title: Zambia Cuts Rates, Bucks Global Pause Fueled by Iran War
item.description: Zambia’s central bank extended its easing cycle as inflation is projected to cool further, even as most policymakers globally adopt a wait-and-see approach to gauge the Iran war’s impact on their forecasts.

item.title: Korean Technocrat Sparks Debate Over ‘Citizen Dividend’ From AI
item.description: A veteran Korean technocrat who built a reputation for handling financial turmoil posted a late-night essay about how the country should use a potential record-breaking tax haul from the AI boom. Soon after the market opened the next morning, it started tanking.

item.title: US Firm Building Cobalt Refinery Signs Congo Supply Agreement
item.description: A company aspiring to build the US’s first large-scale cobalt processing facility announced an agreement that could allow it to source hand-dug metal from the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Srinivasan Pitches Crypto Startup School to Malaysian Officials
item.description: Serial tech investor Balaji Srinivasan is attempting to forge closer ties to politicians in Malaysia, wooing them with a showcase of his startup school project and calling for policies that would support it.

item.title: Raiffeisen Raises Addiko Takeover Offer as Bidding Race Heats Up
item.description: Raiffeisen Bank International AG improved its bid to buy Addiko Bank AG, intensifying the race to buy the Balkans consumer lending specialist.

item.title: India Grabs Russian Oil Before Waiver Expiry as Market Tightens
item.description: Indian imports of Russian oil have been running at a record pace as refiners go all out before a US waiver expires, with processors also broadening their slate of suppliers to cope with the Iran war’s fallout.

item.title: BlackRock Says Increased Downgrades Looming for State Borrowers
item.description: The market for state and local government debt is becoming “less forgiving,” according to BlackRock Inc.’s Pat Haskell, who leads the firm’s municipal bond group.

item.title: BlackRock Says Credit Income Beats Chasing Spreads in Volatility
item.description: BlackRock Inc. says staying invested in credit markets for income is the best way to navigate current market volatility.

item.title: Blackstone Drops $4 Billion New World Deal Over Control Clash
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has walked away from a proposed $4 billion tie-up with New World Development Co. after the embattled property developer refused to hand over the reins, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Transnet to Allow 11 Firms to Operate Trains on Its Rail Lines
item.description: South Africa’s state transport firm Transnet SOC Ltd. granted 11 private companies concessions to operate trains on its freight-rail lines, part of a reform aimed at easing logistical bottlenecks that have constrained economic growth.

item.title: Mideast Markets Diverge as Investors Place Premium on Resilience
item.description: The Iran war has changed how global investors view the Middle East, with geopolitical resilience now outweighing economic growth as a draw for funds.

item.title: Tencent Revenue Miss Heightens Pressure for AI Payoff
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd.’s March-quarter revenue missed estimates, underscoring the urgency around its costly AI pivot while core businesses like games and advertising show signs of age.

item.title: Trump Repeats War Threats Against Iran Ahead of Meeting With Xi
item.description: US President Donald Trump repeated his military threats against Iran ahead of a visit to China, the Islamic Republic’s largest oil customer and a key diplomatic partner.

item.title: Forint Drops After Hungarian Central Bank Cuts Rate on FX Swaps
item.description: The forint weakened after the Hungarian central bank unexpectedly lowered the interest rate on its foreign-currency swaps following one of the biggest emerging-market currency rallies this year.

item.title: Japan Megabanks Set to Win Mythos Access After Bessent Visit
item.description: Japan’s three megabanks are set to secure access to Anthropic’s artificial intelligence model Mythos, according to a person familiar with the matter, after its limited release last month sparked fears of a new age of cybersecurity risks.

item.title: Turkey Depleted Reserves at Record Pace in March Over Iran War
item.description: Turkey’s foreign reserves had their biggest monthly decline on record in March, as the Iran war triggered global selloffs in emerging market assets and strained the lira.

item.title: Italy Says Suspected Hantavirus Case Is Negative, Easing Worries
item.description: A 25-year-old man placed in isolation in Italy tested negative for hantavirus, easing concerns about transmission on a flight that briefly hosted an infected Dutch woman.

item.title: Indonesia Wealth Fund Taps Mandiri Banker Ramadhana as CEO
item.description: Indonesia Investment Authority has hired investment banker Oki Ramadhana as its new chief executive officer, according to people familiar with the matter, filling a leadership vacuum at the country’s first sovereign wealth fund.
